concept: Direct / Guide Toward / Straight and Clear
description: To guide or aim toward a destination; without detour or ambiguity. 'Thenui' names clear guidance—pointing the way, removing confusion, showing the straight path.
sound_symbolism: The word points clearly. 'The' /θe/ begins with clear breath, direction forming. 'Nu' grounds the aim, target established. Finally 'i' arrives with precision, direction clear.
grammatical_notes: An adjective: 'thenui ruela' (direct path), 'thenui phelui' (direct words). Directness, the noun, comes by the quality-noun rule. Directing someone is guiding: see 'kulo' (guide). Related to 'keiro' (way).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Direct teaching transmits without obscuring—clear instruction that doesn't confuse with unnecessary complexity.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Direct communication states clearly—saying what is meant without manipulation or hidden agendas.
